name: schema-visualizer
description: Generate database schema diagrams, ERDs, and documentation from database schemas.
capabilities: Generate Mermaid ERD diagrams from database schemas
Parse Prisma, TypeORM, and SQLAlchemy models
Create schema documentation in Markdown, JSON, and YAML formats
Suggest missing indexes and normalization improvements
Export diagrams for PlantUML and dbdiagram.io
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: All 187 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown text and example code snippets. The scanner incorrectly flagged documentation patterns (bcrypt mentions, command examples, database terms) as security issues. No executable code, network access, filesystem access, or external command execution exists. The pre-computed report correctly assessed this as safe.
valueStatement: Understanding complex database schemas takes time and can lead to mistakes. This skill helps you generate professional ERDs, Mermaid diagrams, and documentation from any database schema source instantly.
userTitle: Generate database schema diagrams and ERDs
limitations: Does not connect to live databases directly
Cannot execute queries or modify database data
Visualization quality depends on schema information provided
Cannot generate diagrams from binary database files

useCases.title: Document existing databases
useCases.description: Generate complete documentation for legacy databases to onboard new team members quickly.

useCases.title: Design and share schemas
useCases.description: Create clear ERDs from ORM models to communicate database designs with stakeholders.

useCases.title: Create schema documentation
useCases.description: Produce readable database documentation in Markdown or JSON format for technical docs.

bestPractices: Provide complete schema information for accurate relationship detection
Use --include-indexes flag to show performance considerations in diagrams
Export to multiple formats to share with different stakeholders
antiPatterns: Assuming the skill can connect to live databases and query data
Providing partial schema information and expecting complete diagrams
Using generated diagrams as the only database documentation

faq.question: Which database systems are supported?
faq.answer: Any database system. The skill works with schema definitions, not specific database engines.

faq.question: What input formats are accepted?
faq.answer: Prisma schemas, TypeORM entities, SQLAlchemy models, SQL dumps, and CREATE TABLE statements.

faq.question: Does this skill access my database?
faq.answer: No. You must provide the schema definition. The skill does not connect to any databases.

faq.question: Can I generate diagrams from multiple ORM formats together?
faq.answer: Yes. Combine multiple schema sources in your prompt for a unified diagram.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Mermaid ERD, PlantUML, dbdiagram.io DBML, Markdown tables, and JSON schema.

faq.question: How does this compare to SchemaSpy or DBeaver?
faq.answer: This skill generates text-based diagrams through AI. Use SchemaSpy or DBeaver for automated database introspection.
